title: andrographis atropurpurea
intro: Andrographis atropurpurea, also known as Kalmegh or Andrographis paniculata, is a flowering plant native to India and Southeast Asia, commonly used in traditional medicine. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities, as well as its ability to modulate the immune system. The health benefits of Andrographis atropurpurea include reducing fever and inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting potential antiviral activity against the common cold and flu. Herbal preparations of Andrographis atropurpurea can be consumed in the form of capsules, tablets, or tea, and are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
